title: Conroe ISD Board Meeting — April 21, 2026

items.title: Discuss Principal Candidate
items.context: The board will meet privately to discuss a candidate for a principal position. Under Texas law (Government Code §551.074), personnel deliberations — including the appointment of school administrators — may be held in closed session to protect the privacy of the individuals involved.

items.title: Update Pursuant to School Board Policy EHBAG (Local)
items.context: The board will receive a legal update from its attorney in closed session related to board policy EHBAG (Local), which pertains to special education or instructional programs for students with disabilities. Closed consultation with legal counsel is permitted when the board's attorney needs to provide advice on matters where open discussion could create legal complications.

items.title: / III.A. Regarding Potential Sale of Property
items.context: The board will privately deliberate the potential sale of district-owned property, which is permitted under Texas law (Government Code §551.072) when open discussion could harm the district's negotiating position. Following closed session, the board will reconvene in open session (Item III.A) to take any formal action on the matter.

items.title: Consider and Approve Minutes
items.context: The board will approve the official minutes from its previous meeting(s), which is a routine administrative action required to maintain an accurate public record of board proceedings.
items.history: Minutes approval is a standard recurring item at every regular board meeting. No unusual prior history noted.

items.title: Consider and Approve Amendment to the Budget
items.context: The board is being asked to approve a change to the district's current operating budget. Budget amendments are common during the school year and typically reflect shifts in spending needs, unexpected costs, or changes in revenue.
items.history: Budget amendments are a recurring item at CISD board meetings throughout the fiscal year. No specific prior history available for this particular amendment.

items.title: Consider on First Reading Board Policy DBD (Local) — Employment Requirements and Restrictions: Conflict of Interest
items.context: This is the first formal review of a proposed update to Policy DBD (Local), which governs rules around conflicts of interest for district employees. A "first reading" means the board is reviewing the policy language but is not yet voting to adopt it — a second reading and vote typically follow at a future meeting.

items.title: Consider on First Reading Board Policy FEA (Local) — Attendance: Compulsory Attendance
items.context: The board is reviewing proposed changes to Policy FEA (Local), which outlines the district's rules regarding compulsory school attendance as required by Texas law. This is a first reading only; no final adoption vote will occur at this meeting.

items.title: Consider on First Reading Board Policy FEC (Local) — Attendance: Attendance for Credit
items.context: This is the first reading of proposed updates to Policy FEC (Local), which governs when students must meet attendance requirements to earn credit for a course. Changes to this policy can affect how absences are handled and what options students have for credit recovery.

items.title: Consider on First Reading Board Policy GC (Local) — Public Notices
items.context: The board is reviewing proposed revisions to Policy GC (Local), which covers how the district communicates public notices to the community. This is a first reading and no final vote will be taken at this meeting.

items.title: Receive Human Resources Report and Consider and Approve Employment of Professional Personnel
items.context: The board will receive a staffing update from Human Resources and vote to formally approve the hiring of professional employees, such as teachers and administrators. This is a routine item required because the board must officially authorize employment of district staff.
items.history: This is a standard recurring item at every regular CISD board meeting. No unusual prior history noted.

items.title: Receive Financial Reports
items.context: The board will review routine financial reports showing the district's current revenue, expenditures, and overall financial position. This is a standard transparency and oversight function of the board.
items.history: Financial reporting is a standard recurring item at every regular board meeting. No unusual prior history noted.

items.title: Approval of the TCEQ Required Water Conservation and Drought Contingency Plan for the Conroe ISD Moorhead/Caney Creek Water Plant Facility
items.context: The Texas Commission on Environmental Quality (TCEQ) requires public water system operators — including school districts that operate their own water facilities — to maintain and regularly update a Water Conservation and Drought Contingency Plan. This item asks the board to approve the plan for CISD's Moorhead/Caney Creek Water Plant, ensuring the district remains in regulatory compliance.

items.title: Recognize Principal for DAEP/JJAEP
items.context: The board will formally recognize the principal serving the District Alternative Education Program (DAEP) and/or the Juvenile Justice Alternative Education Program (JJAEP). These programs serve students who have been removed from the traditional classroom setting, and recognizing their leadership highlights the importance of these programs within CISD.

items.title: Reconsider and Approve the Mascot for Cartwright Junior High
items.context: The board will revisit and take a formal vote on the mascot for Cartwright Junior High School. The word "reconsider" suggests this topic has been discussed or decided upon previously and is being brought back — possibly due to feedback received or a need to formalize the decision.
items.history: No prior history available yet in the provided records, though the use of "reconsider" implies the board has previously discussed this matter.

items.title: Receive Information Regarding Senate Bill 546 Requirement for Bus Seat Belts
items.context: The board will receive a presentation or briefing on Texas Senate Bill 546, which relates to seat belt requirements on school buses. This is an informational item — the board is being updated on a state legislative mandate that may affect district transportation operations and planning.

items.title: Consider and Possibly Adopt District's Virtual School Plan
items.context: The board will review and potentially vote to adopt a formal plan for the district's virtual (online) schooling program. Texas law requires school districts offering virtual instruction to have an approved plan in place governing how those programs operate.

items.title: Receive Information Regarding District Staffing Plan
items.context: District administration will present information about CISD's staffing plan, which may address current or projected staffing levels, hiring needs, or workforce strategy. This is an informational item and no vote is expected.

items.title: Consider and Possibly Approve Library Materials in Accordance with Texas Education Code §33.026, School Board Policy EFB (Local)
items.context: The board will review and potentially vote to approve specific library materials for district libraries, following a process required by Texas Education Code §33.026 and local board policy EFB (Local). This process ensures the board has oversight of the materials available to students in school libraries.

items.title: Consider and Possibly Adopt District's Special Education Homecoming Project Plan
items.context: The board will consider adopting a plan related to a Special Education "Homecoming Project," which appears to be a district initiative focused on students in special education programs. Additional context on the specific nature of this project was not included in the agenda materials.

items.title: Receive Information Regarding The Texas Standard Character Education Program
items.context: The board will receive information about the Texas Standard Character Education Program, a state-supported initiative that incorporates character development into the school curriculum. This is an informational item — no vote is expected at this meeting.

items.title: Receive 2026–2027 Budget Information
items.context: District administration will present early budget information for the upcoming 2026–2027 school year. This is an informational session that typically outlines projected revenues, expenditures, and budget priorities — an important step in the annual budget planning process.
items.history: Early budget presentations for the upcoming fiscal year are a standard part of CISD's spring board meeting cycle. No specific prior history available for the 2026–2027 cycle in the provided records.

items.title: Consider and Approve the Selection of a Construction Manager-at-Risk for The Woodlands 9th Grade and College Park High School Renovations Project
items.context: The board will vote to select a Construction Manager-at-Risk (CMR) — a firm that manages construction while sharing financial risk with the district — for planned renovation work at The Woodlands 9th Grade campus and College Park High School. The board would also authorize the superintendent or designee to negotiate and finalize the contract. This likely involves significant bond or capital funds.

items.title: Consider and Approve the Selection of a Construction Manager-at-Risk for The Auxiliary Facility Renovations Project
items.context: The board will vote on selecting a CMR firm to manage renovations to a district auxiliary facility (which may house support services or operations). The superintendent or designee would be authorized to negotiate and execute the related contracts.

items.title: Consider and Approve the Selection of a Construction Manager-at-Risk for the Caney Creek High School Renovation and District Safety and Security Upgrades Project
items.context: This item asks the board to select a CMR firm to oversee renovations at Caney Creek High School, combined with district-wide safety and security upgrades. The inclusion of safety and security improvements signals this project may be tied to broader campus safety initiatives, potentially bond-funded.

items.title: Consider and Approve the Selection of a Construction Manager-at-Risk for the Anderson and Glen Loch Elementary School Additions and Renovations Project
items.context: The board will vote to select a CMR firm for additions and renovations at Anderson Elementary and Glen Loch Elementary schools. These projects may address capacity, aging facilities, or program space needs at both campuses.

items.title: Consider and Approve Annexation of San Jacinto Elementary Site into MUD 111 and Authorize the Superintendent to Negotiate and Execute Documents
items.context: The board will consider annexing the San Jacinto Elementary School site into Municipal Utility District (MUD) 111, which could affect utility services (such as water and sewer) for that campus. The board would authorize the superintendent to handle the necessary legal and administrative paperwork to complete the transaction.

# Conroe ISD Board Meeting — 2026-04-21
*Regular Meeting — Agenda Breakdown*
*Generated 2026-04-15 22:46 UTC*

---

# Conroe Independent School District — Board of Trustees
## Regular Meeting | Tuesday, April 21, 2026 | 5:00 PM
### Deane L. Sadler Administration Building | 3205 West Davis, Conroe, TX 77304
*Virtual access: http://tiny.conroeisd.net/R78KV*

---

## Procedural Items
The following items are standard meeting procedures and will not be broken down individually:
- **I.A.** Invocation
- **I.B.** Pledge of Allegiance
- **V.** Citizen Participation
- **XII.** Take Requests from Trustees Regarding Future Board Agenda Items
- **XIII.** Adjournment

---

## Closed Session (Section II)

### II.A.1. Discuss Principal Candidate
**Context:** The board will meet privately to discuss a candidate for a principal position. Under Texas law (Government Code §551.074), personnel deliberations — including the appointment of school administrators — may be held in closed session to protect the privacy of the individuals involved.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet.

---

### II.B.1. Update Pursuant to School Board Policy EHBAG (Local)
**Context:** The board will receive a legal update from its attorney in closed session related to board policy EHBAG (Local), which pertains to special education or instructional programs for students with disabilities. Closed consultation with legal counsel is permitted when the board's attorney needs to provide advice on matters where open discussion could create legal complications.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet.

---

### II.C.1. / III.A. Regarding Potential Sale of Property
**Context:** The board will privately deliberate the potential sale of district-owned property, which is permitted under Texas law (Government Code §551.072) when open discussion could harm the district's negotiating position. Following closed session, the board will reconvene in open session (Item III.A) to take any formal action on the matter.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet.

---

## Special Recognition (Section IV)

### IV.A–D. UIL/THSPA State Champions & State Championship Team Recognitions
**Context:** The board will recognize four CISD athletic programs that achieved state-level championships: the Class 6A Powerlifting State Champion teams from Conroe High School, Grand Oaks High School, and The Woodlands High School (UIL/THSPA), as well as the Class 6A Boys Soccer State Championship Team from College Park High School. These recognitions are a standard part of board meetings to celebrate student and campus achievements.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet.

---

## Consent Agenda (Section VI)
*Consent agenda items are typically voted on as a group without individual discussion unless a trustee requests an item be pulled for separate consideration.*

### VI.A. Consider and Approve Minutes
**Context:** The board will approve the official minutes from its previous meeting(s), which is a routine administrative action required to maintain an accurate public record of board proceedings.

**Historical Summary:** Minutes approval is a standard recurring item at every regular board meeting. No unusual prior history noted.

---

### VI.B. Consider and Approve Amendment to the Budget
**Context:** The board is being asked to approve a change to the district's current operating budget. Budget amendments are common during the school year and typically reflect shifts in spending needs, unexpected costs, or changes in revenue.

**Historical Summary:** Budget amendments are a recurring item at CISD board meetings throughout the fiscal year. No specific prior history available for this particular amendment.

---

### VI.C. Consider on First Reading Board Policy DBD (Local) — Employment Requirements and Restrictions: Conflict of Interest
**Context:** This is the first formal review of a proposed update to Policy DBD (Local), which governs rules around conflicts of interest for district employees. A "first reading" means the board is reviewing the policy language but is not yet voting to adopt it — a second reading and vote typically follow at a future meeting.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet.

---

### VI.D. Consider on First Reading Board Policy FEA (Local) — Attendance: Compulsory Attendance
**Context:** The board is reviewing proposed changes to Policy FEA (Local), which outlines the district's rules regarding compulsory school attendance as required by Texas law. This is a first reading only; no final adoption vote will occur at this meeting.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet.

---

### VI.E. Consider on First Reading Board Policy FEC (Local) — Attendance: Attendance for Credit
**Context:** This is the first reading of proposed updates to Policy FEC (Local), which governs when students must meet attendance requirements to earn credit for a course. Changes to this policy can affect how absences are handled and what options students have for credit recovery.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet.

---

### VI.F. Consider on First Reading Board Policy GC (Local) — Public Notices
**Context:** The board is reviewing proposed revisions to Policy GC (Local), which covers how the district communicates public notices to the community. This is a first reading and no final vote will be taken at this meeting.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet.

---

### VI.G. Receive Human Resources Report and Consider and Approve Employment of Professional Personnel
**Context:** The board will receive a staffing update from Human Resources and vote to formally approve the hiring of professional employees, such as teachers and administrators. This is a routine item required because the board must officially authorize employment of district staff.

**Historical Summary:** This is a standard recurring item at every regular CISD board meeting. No unusual prior history noted.

---

### VI.H. Receive Financial Reports
**Context:** The board will review routine financial reports showing the district's current revenue, expenditures, and overall financial position. This is a standard transparency and oversight function of the board.

**Historical Summary:** Financial reporting is a standard recurring item at every regular board meeting. No unusual prior history noted.

---

### VI.I. Approval of the TCEQ Required Water Conservation and Drought Contingency Plan for the Conroe ISD Moorhead/Caney Creek Water Plant Facility
**Context:** The Texas Commission on Environmental Quality (TCEQ) requires public water system operators — including school districts that operate their own water facilities — to maintain and regularly update a Water Conservation and Drought Contingency Plan. This item asks the board to approve the plan for CISD's Moorhead/Caney Creek Water Plant, ensuring the district remains in regulatory compliance.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet.

---

## Human Resources (Section VII)

### VII.A. Recognize Principal for DAEP/JJAEP
**Context:** The board will formally recognize the principal serving the District Alternative Education Program (DAEP) and/or the Juvenile Justice Alternative Education Program (JJAEP). These programs serve students who have been removed from the traditional classroom setting, and recognizing their leadership highlights the importance of these programs within CISD.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet.

---

## Administration (Section VIII)

### VIII.A. Reconsider and Approve the Mascot for Cartwright Junior High
**Context:** The board will revisit and take a formal vote on the mascot for Cartwright Junior High School. The word "reconsider" suggests this topic has been discussed or decided upon previously and is being brought back — possibly due to feedback received or a need to formalize the decision.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet in the provided records, though the use of "reconsider" implies the board has previously discussed this matter.

---

### VIII.B. Receive Information Regarding Senate Bill 546 Requirement for Bus Seat Belts
**Context:** The board will receive a presentation or briefing on Texas Senate Bill 546, which relates to seat belt requirements on school buses. This is an informational item — the board is being updated on a state legislative mandate that may affect district transportation operations and planning.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet.

---

### VIII.C. Consider and Possibly Adopt District's Virtual School Plan
**Context:** The board will review and potentially vote to adopt a formal plan for the district's virtual (online) schooling program. Texas law requires school districts offering virtual instruction to have an approved plan in place governing how those programs operate.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet.

---

### VIII.D. Receive Information Regarding District Staffing Plan
**Context:** District administration will present information about CISD's staffing plan, which may address current or projected staffing levels, hiring needs, or workforce strategy. This is an informational item and no vote is expected.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet.

---

## Teaching and Learning (Section IX)

### IX.A. Consider and Possibly Approve Library Materials in Accordance with Texas Education Code §33.026, School Board Policy EFB (Local)
**Context:** The board will review and potentially vote to approve specific library materials for district libraries, following a process required by Texas Education Code §33.026 and local board policy EFB (Local). This process ensures the board has oversight of the materials available to students in school libraries.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet.

---

### IX.B. Consider and Possibly Adopt District's Special Education Homecoming Project Plan
**Context:** The board will consider adopting a plan related to a Special Education "Homecoming Project," which appears to be a district initiative focused on students in special education programs. Additional context on the specific nature of this project was not included in the agenda materials.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet.

---

### IX.C. Receive Information Regarding The Texas Standard Character Education Program
**Context:** The board will receive information about the Texas Standard Character Education Program, a state-supported initiative that incorporates character development into the school curriculum. This is an informational item — no vote is expected at this meeting.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet.

---

## Business/Finance (Section X)

### X.A. Receive 2026–2027 Budget Information
**Context:** District administration will present early budget information for the upcoming 2026–2027 school year. This is an informational session that typically outlines projected revenues, expenditures, and budget priorities — an important step in the annual budget planning process.

**Historical Summary:** Early budget presentations for the upcoming fiscal year are a standard part of CISD's spring board meeting cycle. No specific prior history available for the 2026–2027 cycle in the provided records.

---

## Planning and Construction (Section XI)

### XI.A. Consider and Approve the Selection of a Construction Manager-at-Risk for The Woodlands 9th Grade and College Park High School Renovations Project
**Context:** The board will vote to select a Construction Manager-at-Risk (CMR) — a firm that manages construction while sharing financial risk with the district — for planned renovation work at The Woodlands 9th Grade campus and College Park High School. The board would also authorize the superintendent or designee to negotiate and finalize the contract. This likely involves significant bond or capital funds.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet.

---

### XI.B. Consider and Approve the Selection of a Construction Manager-at-Risk for The Auxiliary Facility Renovations Project
**Context:** The board will vote on selecting a CMR firm to manage renovations to a district auxiliary facility (which may house support services or operations). The superintendent or designee would be authorized to negotiate and execute the related contracts.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet.

---

### XI.C. Consider and Approve the Selection of a Construction Manager-at-Risk for the Caney Creek High School Renovation and District Safety and Security Upgrades Project
**Context:** This item asks the board to select a CMR firm to oversee renovations at Caney Creek High School, combined with district-wide safety and security upgrades. The inclusion of safety and security improvements signals this project may be tied to broader campus safety initiatives, potentially bond-funded.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet.

---

### XI.D. Consider and Approve the Selection of a Construction Manager-at-Risk for the Anderson and Glen Loch Elementary School Additions and Renovations Project
**Context:** The board will vote to select a CMR firm for additions and renovations at Anderson Elementary and Glen Loch Elementary schools. These projects may address capacity, aging facilities, or program space needs at both campuses.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet.

---

### XI.E. Consider and Approve Annexation of San Jacinto Elementary Site into MUD 111 and Authorize the Superintendent to Negotiate and Execute Documents
**Context:** The board will consider annexing the San Jacinto Elementary School site into Municipal Utility District (MUD) 111, which could affect utility services (such as water and sewer) for that campus. The board would authorize the superintendent to handle the necessary legal and administrative paperwork to complete the transaction.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available yet.

---

*This breakdown was prepared for informational purposes to help constituents understand the business before the Conroe ISD Board of Trustees. For official agenda documents and supporting materials, visit the CISD public web portal.*
